print(f"White bar pixels: {seg['nodes'][13]['pixels']}")
</parameter>

</tool_call>

Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the whole level. In level 1, there was a purple square that moved along a diagonal path. The goal was to move it to the correct position. In level 2, maybe the goal is similar but with multiple dots.
Looking at the sky blue dots, they form two diagonal arms extending from the blue shape. Maybe the goal is to move these dots along their diagonal paths until they reach the blue shape.
But I already tried clicking on the dots and they turned magenta and then moved up-left. That didn't work because I ran out of time.
Let me try a different approach: maybe I should only click on ONE dot at a time and see if it moves toward the blue shape. Or maybe I should click on the blue shape first to "activate" it, then click on the dots.
